text:
MRI OROPHARYNX AND NECK WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST
For Treatment Planning

CLINICAL INDICATION: 52-year-old female with newly diagnosed HPV-positive (p16+) squamous cell carcinoma of the right base of tongue, T2N1M0 (AJCC 8th edition). Referred by radiation oncology for high-resolution MRI for IMRT treatment planning and gross tumor volume delineation. Prior CT neck with contrast and staging FDG-PET/CT available for correlation.

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the oropharynx and neck obtained on a 3.0 Tesla scanner. Sequences acquired include axial T1, axial and coronal T2 fat-saturated, diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with apparent diffusion coefficient (ADC) mapping, and post-gadolinium axial and coronal T1 fat-saturated sequences following IV administration of gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg. Dedicated thin-slice (2 mm) axial sequences through the oropharynx obtained for contouring purposes. Patient supine with thermoplastic immobilization mask in treatment position. Examination performed in close coordination with radiation oncology treatment planning team.

---

FINDINGS:

PRIMARY TUMOR — RIGHT BASE OF TONGUE:
There is a well-defined, heterogeneously T2-hyperintense mass centered within the right base of tongue measuring approximately 3.0 cm in greatest axial dimension (craniocaudal extent approximately 2.8 cm, transverse 3.0 cm, anteroposterior 2.5 cm). On post-contrast T1-weighted sequences, the lesion demonstrates moderate, heterogeneous enhancement with areas of relative non-enhancement internally, consistent with the known histology of moderately differentiated squamous cell carcinoma. DWI demonstrates restricted diffusion within the mass (ADC values approximately 0.85 x 10^-3 mm^2/s), in keeping with high cellularity.

The tumor is centered in the right posterolateral tongue base, with lateral extension to involve the right glossoepiglottic fold. The mucosa overlying the lingual surface of the right tonsillar fossa shows contiguous involvement at its inferior margin, though the right palatine tonsil itself appears spared. There is no convincing involvement of the right glossotonsillar sulcus or the right lateral pharyngeal wall.

Important negative findings regarding extent of disease:
- The tongue midline (median fibrous septum) is preserved; no crossing of midline is identified on any sequence.
- The vallecula and lingual surface of the epiglottis are uninvolved. The pre-epiglottic space fat signal is maintained without any infiltration.
- The hyoepiglottic ligament and its adjacent fat are intact.
- The extrinsic tongue musculature (hyoglossus, styloglossus, and genioglossus on the right) shows no signal abnormality to suggest invasion; fatty planes between the tumor and the adjacent musculature, while slightly compressed, appear preserved on the most medial aspect.
- The pterygoid musculature (medial and lateral) bilaterally appears normal in signal and morphology. No involvement of the parapharyngeal space. The parapharyngeal fat stripe on the right is intact.
- The mandible is unremarkable; no evidence of cortical erosion, medullary infiltration, or periosteal reaction on any sequence. Fat signal of the mandibular marrow is normal bilaterally.
- Posterior pharyngeal wall is not involved.
- The floor of mouth, oral tongue, and soft palate are within normal limits.

REGIONAL LYMPH NODES:
Right neck: There is an enlarged right level IIA cervical lymph node measuring approximately 2.4 cm in short-axis dimension (previously identified on CT). On MRI, this node demonstrates heterogeneous internal signal on T2 and irregular peripheral enhancement on post-contrast sequences with a mildly thickened rim and areas of central low T2 signal potentially reflecting necrosis or dense tumor. Restricted diffusion is present within this node (ADC approximately 0.90 x 10^-3 mm^2/s). The extranodal extension criterion — defined as irregular marginal enhancement with adjacent soft tissue stranding — is carefully assessed; the nodal capsule appears overall intact with no frank spiculation or infiltration into adjacent soft tissue planes, though the imaging appearance is suspicious. No definitive frank extranodal extension is identified, though this remains a close call given the irregular enhancement.

Additional right-sided level IIA and IIB nodes are within normal limits (short-axis <1 cm, homogeneous enhancement). No pathologically suspicious right level III, IV, or level V lymph nodes.

Left neck: No pathologically enlarged or morphologically suspicious cervical lymph nodes on the left. The largest visible left-sided node measures 0.7 cm in short axis with normal fatty hilum preservation.

Retropharyngeal nodes: No enlarged or suspicious retropharyngeal lymph nodes are identified.

VASCULAR STRUCTURES:
The carotid arteries are patent bilaterally. The right internal carotid artery shows no evidence of encasement or direct tumor contact. The internal jugular veins are patent bilaterally.

SALIVARY GLANDS:
Both parotid glands appear normal. The submandibular glands are normal bilaterally. No obstructive salivary pathology identified.

THYROID GLAND:
The thyroid gland is homogeneous in signal without focal nodularity. No goiter or suspicious thyroid lesion identified.

AIRWAY:
The hypopharyngeal airway and supraglottic larynx are patent. The true and false vocal cords are symmetric in appearance with no subglottic involvement. The oropharyngeal airway is mildly narrowed on the right by the base of tongue mass but patent.

CRANIAL NERVE ASSESSMENT (limited):
No perineural spread along the lingual nerve or inferior alveolar nerve branches. The hypoglossal canal appears symmetric without expansion. No skull base involvement.

POST-PROCESSING NOTES:
Tumor gross volume estimation for radiation planning purposes: The primary tumor volumetrics will be provided to radiation oncology separately. Tumor contouring datasets have been transferred to the treatment planning workstation. Correlation with prior FDG-PET/CT (SUVmax 12.4, right base of tongue; SUVmax 8.7 right level IIA node) is recommended for integrated GTV definition.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. T2-hyperintense, heterogeneously enhancing right base of tongue mass measuring approximately 3.0 cm, consistent with the known squamous cell carcinoma. The mass involves the right glossoepiglottic fold. No involvement of the tongue base midline, pre-epiglottic space, vallecula, epiglottis, parapharyngeal space, pterygoid musculature, or mandible.

2. Right level IIA cervical lymph node measuring 2.4 cm with irregular internal enhancement and restricted diffusion, consistent with the known nodal metastasis. No definitive frank extranodal extension, though the imaging appearance warrants careful clinical correlation. No contralateral or bilateral lymphadenopathy.

3. No involvement of critical structures that would substantially alter surgical or radiation planning approach. Findings support locoregional T2N1 disease without evidence of distant spread on this examination.

4. High-resolution imaging dataset transferred to radiation oncology treatment planning workstation for IMRT gross tumor volume and clinical target volume delineation.

Dictated and reviewed by: Diagnostic Radiology / Neuroradiology
Clinical correlation recommended.
